title: tephrosia albissima subsp. zuluensis
intro: Tephrosia albissima subsp. zuluensis is a subspecies of plant belonging to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pea family. This plant is traditionally used in South Africa for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits associated with Tephrosia albissima subsp. zuluensis include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a purgative and diuretic. Traditional herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which are often used to treat a range of health issues.
